ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002694 covers french fries as a category; no size-specific (medium) variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers fried potato products broadly; no medium-serving-size specific code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226803007 covers french fried potato as a food concept; no size-specific variant exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium serving of fast-food french fries is approximately 117 g, equivalent to a standard medium-size carton as served at quick-service restaurants such as Jollibee.

nutrient_source.title: Potatoes, french fried, all types, salt added in processing, frozen, as purchased (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 11400 / FDC ID 170697)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for frozen french fries (salt added, as purchased) used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values represent the fried, salted potato strip product typical of fast-food medium fries. Serving size of 117 g is consistent with published fast-food medium fry weights. Minor adaptation: per-100g values derived directly from USDA data; no further adjustment applied.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; French fries (deep-fried potato strips)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 75 (high) is drawn from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283), which report french fries at GI ~75 (glucose reference = 100). This is applied as the closest analogous estimate for a standard fast-food medium fries serving. GL per serving = (75 × 35.7 × 1.17) / 100 = 31.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (210 mg per 100 g; ~246 mg per medium serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit medium fries consumption due to moderate sodium content (~246 mg per serving); frequent intake may contribute to exceeding the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 75) and high glycemic load per serving (~31 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit medium fries due to a high GI of 75 and a glycemic load of approximately 31 per medium serving, which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat (13.1 g/100 g), saturated fat (2.3 g/100 g), and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume medium fries sparingly, as the combination of total fat, saturated fat, and residual trans fat from frying oil contributes to atherogenic lipid burden when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Medium French Fries are a standard fast-food side dish consisting of potato strips that have been deep-fried in vegetable oil and salted. A medium serving typically weighs approximately 117 g and is served in a cardboard carton at quick-service restaurants such as Jollibee, McDonald's, and similar chains. The fries are golden-brown in colour, crispy on the outside, and soft on the inside, with a characteristic savoury, salty flavour derived from the frying process and added salt. In Singapore, medium fries are widely available at fast-food outlets and are commonly consumed as a snack or side dish accompanying burgers and fried chicken meals. Nutritionally, a medium serving provides approximately 321 kcal, 41.8 g of carbohydrate, 15.3 g of fat, and 4.0 g of protein. The glycemic index is high (GI ~75), making this food a significant concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance, as it can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation. The sodium content (~246 mg per serving) is moderate and relevant for individuals with hypertension. The presence of saturated and trans fats from deep-frying is a consideration for cardiovascular health. Potassium (579 mg/100 g) and vitamin B6 (0.29 mg/100 g) are notable micronutrients contributed by the potato base. This food is halal, vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free in its basic formulation, though cross-contamination risks may apply in shared fryer environments.
common_user_queries: How many calories in medium fries?
How much sodium in medium fries?
What is the GI of french fries?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for frozen french fries (salt added), which is the closest approved source for a generic fast-food medium fries product. The specific Jollibee Singapore medium fries formulation is not available in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded). USDA SR Legacy data is used as an adapted reference. GI is drawn from the University of Sydney GI Database via the Atkinson et al. 2008 international tables, applied as an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Food identity: The input record uses the name 'Medium Fries (D)' from field survey data / Jollibee Bugis Village. The canonical English name has been normalised to 'Medium French Fries'; 'Medium Fries (D)' is retained as an alias. The '(D)' suffix likely denotes a menu variant code and is not part of the canonical food name. 2. Nutrient source: No Jollibee Singapore-specific nutrition panel is available in any approved primary source. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170697, frozen french fries, salt added, as purchased) is used as the closest approved analogue. Values are per 100 g as reported by USDA. 3. Serving size: 117 g is used as the medium serving weight, consistent with published fast-food medium fry serving weights in the literature and USDA reference data. 4. Cholesterol: cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; french fries made from potato and vegetable oil contain no dietary cholesterol. 5. Vitamin A: vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this product type. 6. Vitamin B12: vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; plant-based fried potato product contains no vitamin B12. 7. Vitamin D: vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this product type. 8. Sentinel values (-1): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all populated with numeric values from USDA FoodData Central and are NOT set to -1 in this record. All per_100g fields have been populated with USDA-sourced numeric values where available. 9. Multilingual names: This food is classified as Fast Food served in Singapore; Singaporean multilingual naming rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been applied. 10. Halal status: Jollibee Singapore is listed as a Halal-certified restaurant on the MUIS Singapore Halal certification register; is_halal set to true. 11. GI calculation: GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_100g × serving_size_g) / 100 = (75 × 35.7 × 1.17) / 100 = 31.4 g.
